mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >55% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an abrupt slowdown in consumer spending capacity, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in >60% of instances following a >0.4pp unemployment spike, or signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects improving manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which translates into higher corporate profit margins and positive equity returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to generate positive 20-day returns during recovery phases, or the signal reverses direction when inflation expectations exceed 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly correlates with stronger corporate earnings trajectories, producing immediate positive market repricing and positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no predictive power for 5-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01) across rolling 2-year windows, or returns are negative in >55% of high-GDP periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above 3.5% forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy stances, increasing borrowing costs and compressing equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of periods when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.5%, or the inflation threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm robust labor market tightening, boosting consumer confidence and corporate hiring, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 20-day returns in >50% of instances, or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing industrial production and stable GDP growth indicates supply chain bottlenecks and input cost inflation, eroding profit margins and triggering negative S&P 500 returns within 5 days.
failure_condition: INDPRO deceleration alongside stable GDPC1 fails to predict negative 5-day returns, or the signal shows positive predictive power instead of negative during supply-constrained periods.
